mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals ongoing labor market expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals a tight labor market that generates wage-push inflation expectations and compresses profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.5% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal a binding monetary policy response that raises real discount rates and dampens equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month increase in both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not show a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.05) in a 20-day forward return regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening residential investment and downstream demand for durables, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when the economy is recovering from a prior demand contraction.
failure_condition: During periods labeled as post-recession demand recovery, HOUST increases do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window within that regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving manufacturing momentum that translates into immediate positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO accelerations are not positive in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.0% signals a restrictive monetary stance that raises borrowing costs and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% during walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong retail sales (RSAFS) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a robust consumer demand environment that boosts corporate revenues and margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month increase in RSAFS concurrent with UNRATE below 5%
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and UNRATE does not produce a positive and significant relationship with 5-day forward returns (interaction term p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating quarter-over-quarter growth rate in real GDP (GDPC1) signals broad-based demand expansion that lifts expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 accelerations are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
